title: Bachelor's Degree Candidates' Ceremony
description: Join the commencement class of 2026 as they celebrate the culmination of their achievements at CCA.
longDescription: California College of the Arts proudly presents its 119th Commencement, honoring the extraordinary achievements of our undergraduate Class of 2026. This afternoon ceremony celebrates the artists, designers, architects, and writers who have shaped their creative voices over the past four years. Join family, friends, faculty, and fellow graduates for a memorable afternoon of recognition, reflection, and celebration.

image.alt: Graduates in caps, gowns, and cultural regalia stand in an auditorium with red seats, many wearing decorative sashes and stoles representing their cultural heritage.

studentSpeaker.name: Priya Anand
studentSpeaker.speechHighlights: Priya will reflect on the power of visual storytelling to bridge cultures, inspired by her thesis project exploring diaspora narratives through sequential art.

programItems.label: Grand marshal
programItems.presenter: Professor Elena Vasquez, Professor Emerita of Sculpture

ceremonyProgram.landAcknowledgment: California College of the Arts acknowledges that our campus is located on the unceded territory of Chochenyo and Karkin Ohlone peoples, who have stewarded this land throughout the generations. We recognize their continuing connection to the land and pay our respects to their elders past and present.
ceremonyProgram.disclaimer: The list of degree candidates represents those who have applied for graduation. Inclusion in the commencement program does not confirm that a degree has been conferred. Degrees are officially conferred only upon completion of all academic requirements as certified by the Registrar.
